channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple denies wrongdoing but settles to pay $95 million cash (about $20 per class member) in Siri privacy suit
item.description: Apple agreed to pay $95 million in cash to settle a proposed class action lawsuit claiming that its voice-activated Siri assistant violated users’ privacy. A preliminary settlement was filed on Tuesday night in the Oakland, California federal court, and requires approval by U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Apple Settles Lawsuit Over Alleged Eavesdropping with Siri - $95 Million Settlement
item.description: Apple agrees to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit alleging the company's virtual assistant, Siri, eavesdropped on iPhone users without consent.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Predicting what 2025 will mean for AI
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: Your new AirTag has a hidden warning that's just for kids – and it's for the best
item.description: Apple's AirTag is now in compliance with US's Reese's Law, according to the government's consumer product safety watchdog.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Joel Kaplan to replace Nick Clegg as Meta global policy leader
item.description: Meta will switch up its global policy team in the new year and has appointed former George W. Bush administration official Joel Kaplan to replace Nick Clegg as the company's chief global affairs officer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Future of Health: Apple's AI-Driven Approach to Public Well-being
item.description: Apple's strategic approach to artificial intelligence deployment could make a radical difference in public health, but concerns around data privacy remain a major challenge.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 6 big entertainment stories you missed over the 2024 holiday season: The Batman Part 2 delayed again, The Night Agent season 2 trailer, and more
item.description: From new TV show trailers to some DC movie reveals, here are the biggest news stories you missed over the 2024 holiday season.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: US government says companies are no longer allowed to send bulk data to these nations
item.description: The US DoJ has issued a final rule to prevent the mass transfer of US citizen data to hostile nations.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: What is Turo? Car rental app used in New Orleans and Las Vegas
item.description: Turo, a San Francisco-based company founded in 2010, operates differently from traditional car rental services.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: 12 must-have apps to crush your New Year’s resolutions
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you crush your New Year's goals with these 12 apps to help you stay on track and succeed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 4 keys for writing cross-platform apps
item.description: These days, most every application has the potential to be a cross-platform application. That’s great for users but often difficult for developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rhode Island’s state health benefits website hacked, user data exposed
item.description: Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: The vital role of red teaming in safeguarding AI systems and data
item.description: Companies are feeling pressure to adopt generative AI to stay ahead in a competitive global market. To ensure they adopt responsibly, governing and regulatory bodies across the world are convening to debate and understand the best ways to address AI risk without stifling their innovation edge.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Honey browser extension accused of scamming influencers, users
item.description: In the the 23-minute video by user MegaLag, the PayPal property is accused of abusing the affiliate link system for its own profit.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Small businesses brace themselves for potentially disruptive TikTok ban
item.description: A looming TikTok ban could affect the millions of small businesses that use the short-video social media app to grow their business.
item.dc:creator: Mae Anderson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The long-term impacts of AI on networking
item.description: AI is either going to spawn a golden age of profits and benefits or kill us all off, according to popular viewpoints on the subject. The latter view has been at least somewhat discredited; if you yell “AI” in a crowded theater these days, hardly anyone runs for the exits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Travel Troubleshooter: Error message occurs when using Chase points for travel
item.description: When Carlye Siegel tries to book two award tickets with Chase points, the transaction fails. Now Chase won't refund the points.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Elliott, King Features Syndicate, Inc., The Mercury News

item.title: How to restore deleted text messages on Android
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals ways to restore accidentally deleted text messages on your Android phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hands-on with Adobe’s Acrobat AI Assistant: AI for your PDFs
item.description: We may be on the brink of 2025, but PDFs are still unavoidable in the professional world. No matter what industry you work in, you’re bound to whittle away precious moments wading through reports, white papers, and other dense documents in that clunky-feeling form.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 10 hot programming trends
item.description: Tides ebb and flow. Pendulums swing. Ideas come and go, especially in programming, where the technologies and techniques for creating software continuously evolve. Some programming languages, libraries, platforms, and tools become hot and irresistible.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk causes uproar by backing Germany’s far-right party AfD
item.description: The Tesla Motors CEO also wrote that his investment in Germany gave him the right to comment on the country’s condition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: How the H1-B visa is causing strife within Trump’s coalition
item.description: An online spat between factions of Donald Trump’s supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in his political movement into public display, previewing the fissures and contradictory views his coalition could bring to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors
item.description: Learn how rich text editors help government agencies ensure compliance with data security, accessibility, and document standardization. The post Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they're using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Critical warning from Microsoft: .NET install domains changing
item.description: Users who expected to be able to use the resources of the Azure content delivery network (CDN) from Edgio until November 2025, as previously announced, had a not-so-festive “gift” this week – the CDN is now shutting down on Jan. 15, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Modern ICAM strategies: Real-time identity management across agencies, partners and at the tactical edge
item.description: Gain insights into the future of ICAM and its role in secure, modern government operations. The post Modern ICAM strategies: Real-time identity management across agencies, partners and at the tactical edge first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk stirs pot on X over 'shortage' of talented American engineers
item.description: Musk, an immigrant from South Africa, posted to X that there is a shortage of "talented" engineers in the U.S. and companies should start looking to other countries.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation
